data.title: Silver tumbles into bear market as $70 breaks, ‘rug pull’ fears grow
data.description:
Silver slid below $70, extending a sharp downturn that has pushed the metal into bear market territory after a steep reversal from early-March highs.

Prices are now down nearly 30% since March 2, with losses accelerating as the iShares Silver Trust (SLV) broke below its 100-day moving average, a key technical support level that traders say has triggered systematic selling.

Market focus has also turned to shifts in price-setting dynamics.

“Warned a month ago that Jane Street becoming the biggest marginal price setter guarantees a rug pull,” Zerohedge said in a post on X, highlighting concerns that liquidity dominated by high-frequency trading firms can amplify reversals once momentum fades.

Analysts said the speed of the decline points more to positioning being unwound than a deterioration in underlying fundamentals. With liquidity thinning and volatility rising, the breakdown in technical levels suggests further downside risk unless prices stabilize quickly.

The latest move underscores how flow-driven rallies can reverse abruptly when key participants step back, leaving markets vulnerable to sharp air-pocket declines.

data.title: Hilton adds YOTEL properties to 'Select by Hilton' platform
data.description:
As part of its asset-light model, Hilton (HLT) has added independently owned YOTEL properties to its “Select by Hilton” portfolio.

YOTEL operates “highly efficient” hotels in urban markets, filling a customer need in the growing lifestyle segment. The company will continue to independently manage and license its 23 hotels in 10 countries while enabling Hilton (HLT) to expand its portfolio without adding company-owned or franchise properties.

As part of Hilton’s newly established “Select by Hilton” brand, YOTEL can retain its identity while at the same time being connected to the  Hilton Honors loyalty program and its platform of global properties. This would enable YOTEL guests to accumulate Hilton Honors points and enjoy benefits of the Hilton (HLT) brand. 

“YOTEL’s relationship with Hilton allows us to expand our reach while staying true to who we are. What changes for YOTEL is access—not identity—in a capital-light and scalable way,” said YOTEL’s CEO, Phil Andreopoulos.

data.title: DDC Enterprise adds 200 Bitcoin, lifting total holdings to 2.38K BTC
data.description:
• DDC Enterprise (DDC) recently purchased 200 Bitcoin (BTC-USD), increasing its total holdings to 2.38K BTC, while securing the 32nd global rank among public corporate Bitcoin holders.
  • Following this, total holdings now stand at 2.38K BTC at an average cost of ~$79.97K per BTC, with a YTD yield of 44.9% and 0.057626 BTC per 1K DDC shares.
  • The company plans to use BTC as its primary reserve asset and continue buying as part of its Bitcoin accumulation strategy.

data.title: Accenture rises after Q2 beat, revises outlook
data.description:
Shares of Accenture (ACN) rose about 3% on Thursday after fiscal second quarter results beat estimates, despite third quarter revenue coming in below expectations.

The company also raised the lower end of its full fiscal year 2026 revenue and adjusted EPS outlook.

Q2 Metrics 

For the fiscal second quarter ended Feb. 28, revenue rose 8% in U.S. dollar terms (4% in local currency) year-over-year to $18.04B, while GAAP EPS climbed about 4% year-on-year to $2.93. Both top and bottom line numbers surpassed analysts' expectations.

"We delivered record second quarter bookings of $22.1 billion, including a record 41 clients with quarterly bookings greater than $100 million, with revenues at the top of our guided range, while continuing to take significant share in a competitive market," said Accenture's Chair and CEO Julie Sweet.

The company said new bookings for the second quarter of fiscal 2026 were $22.11B, an increase of 6% in U.S. dollars and 1% in local currency compared to the second quarter of fiscal 2025.

The Irish company's Consulting revenues in the first quarter grew 7% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year to $8.86B, while Managed Services revenues jumped 10% (in U.S. dollars) to $9.18B.

Americas was the top geographic market by revenue, generating $8.90B in the fiscal second quarter, growing 4% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year.

The company had a free cash flow of $3.7B.

Dividend: Accenture said it has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.63 per share for shareholders of record at the close of business on April 9. This dividend, which is payable on May 15, represents a 10% increase over the quarterly dividend rate of $1.48 per share in fiscal 2025.

Stock Buyback: During the second quarter of fiscal 2026, Accenture bought back or redeemed 6.8M shares for a total of $1.7B, including 5.1M shares repurchased in the open market. Accenture’s total remaining share repurchase authority at Feb. 28 was about $4.4B.

Outlook

Accenture said its third-quarter and full-year 2026 business outlook reflect the company’s best view of the potential impact of the conflict in the Middle East in the second half of this fiscal year. It does not take into account a significant escalation or the occurrence of major economic disruption.

The company expects fiscal third quarter revenue to be between $18.35B and $19B (midpoint at $18.675B), versus a consensus revenue estimate of $18.73B. 

The guidance assumes a foreign exchange impact of about +2.5% versus the third quarter of fiscal 2025. It implies 1% to 5% local-currency growth, including about a 1% impact from federal business. Excluding federal, a 2% to 6% local-currency growth.

For the full-year fiscal 2026, Accenture now expects revenue growth (in local currency) to be in the range of 3% to 5%, versus a prior forecast of 2% to 5%. The guidance includes an estimated 1% impact from federal business; excluding federal, 4% to 6% local-currency growth.

The foreign exchange impact on results in USD is expected to be about +2% versus fiscal year 2025. 

The company now expects adjusted EPS to be between $13.65 and $13.90 (midpoint at $13.775) versus a prior forecast of $13.52 and $13.90 (midpoint at $13.71), compared to a consensus EPS estimate of $13.86.

Acquisitions: The company now expects to invest about $5B in acquisitions, with the capacity to do more if opportunities arise.

Accenture raised fiscal 2026 free cash flow guidance by $1B. The company now expects free cash flow to be between $10.8B and $11.5B, versus a prior forecast of $9.8B to $10.5B, reflecting a free cash flow to net income ratio of 1.3.

Full-year 2026 adjusted operating margin is expected to be 15.7% to 15.9%, representing 10 bps to 30 bps expansion over adjusted FY 2025 results.

The company said the fiscal year 2026 operating cash flow expected range is $11.5B to $12.2B.

Fiscal year 2026 property and equipment additions are now expected to be about $700M.

Accenture continues to expect to return at least $9.3B through dividends and share repurchases, an increase of $1B, or 12%, from fiscal year 2025.

Update: The story was updated with additional information on guidance and stock price movement.

data.title: Wall Street slides as talks on inflation fears rise
data.description:
Wall Street tumbled on Thursday as traders digested more economic data and assessed the Federal Reserve's latest policy decision.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.9%, the Dow (DJI) -0.7%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -1.2%.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher following the inflation data and Fed announcement. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1.8 basis points to 4.28%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) climbed 3.5 basis points to 3.81%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) ticked up 0.7 basis points to 4.89%.

The Fed issued its decision to keep interest rates unchanged within a range of 3.5%-3.75%, as expected.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell said it is important to keep interest rates mildly restrictive, and the Fed added a new sentence to its guidance stating that the economic implications of the conflict in the Middle East were “uncertain.” 

"The price action screams of a market that’s run out of puff and maybe poised for protracted downside. Higher inflation and lower growth stemming from an energy crisis in the Middle East are kryptonite for equity prices. Meanwhile, slightly out of the limelight, there are growing signs of financial stress in the system stemming from private credit markets that could also weaken public markets," Kyle Rodda from capital.com said. 

The Philly Fed Manufacturing Index unexpectedly rose in March, while the initial jobless claims unexpectedly dropped in the past week.

data.title: UK Gilt yields skyrocket on BOE pause, U.S. Treasuries follow as rate cut hopes fade
data.description:
Global sovereign bond yields climbed sharply on Thursday, led by a pronounced move in UK government debt after the Bank of England opted to hold interest rates steady, signaling persistent concern over inflation risks tied in part to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

The Bank of England voted unanimously 9-0 to maintain its benchmark rate at 3.75%, a decision that prompted an immediate repricing across the gilt market. 

Short-dated yields bore the brunt of the move, with the UK 2-Year Treasury yield surging 35 basis points to 4.45%, its highest level since January 2025 and a fresh 52-week high. Longer maturities followed suit, as the UK 5-year Treasury yield jumped roughly 25 basis points to 4.52%, while the UK 10-year Treasury yield climbed 15 basis points to 4.89%. 

The upward pressure on yields was not confined to Great Britain. In the United States, Treasury yields also advanced, particularly at the front end of the curve. The U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose by 8 basis points, reaching its highest level in seven months after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell signaled a more cautious stance on rate cuts. 

Together, the moves reflect a broader global shift in rate expectations, as investors adjust to the prospect of tighter monetary policy persisting for longer than previously anticipated. U.S. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

U.K. Focused ETFs: (EWU), (FLGB), (FKU), (FXB), and (EWUS).

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan seeks permit for $7.5B Chile mine expansion
data.description:
Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) said Thursday it submitted a request for an environmental permit for a $7.5B expansion that would quadruple output at ​its El Abra copper ​mine in Chile.

Freeport (FCX) said the overhaul includes new concentrator and desalination plants and tailings storage, and potentially boosting annual copper production by more than 300K metric tons, with expanded operations expected to start in 2033.

While a final investment decision is still pending, Freeport (FCX) executives briefed Chile's new Economy and Mining Minister Daniel Mas in Santiago on Thursday, Bloomberg reported.

Freeport (FCX) operates El Abra and has a 51% stake, while state-owned Codelco holds the remaining 49%.

Freeport (FCX) shares are down ~5% pre-market Thursday as copper futures continue to decline, with the widening Middle East war lifting energy prices and increasing the risk of damage to the global economy.

Southern Copper (SCCO), Teck Resources (TECK), Taseko Mines (TGB) and Hudbay Minerals (HBM) are all indicated sharply lower.

ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), (JJCTF)

data.title: Micron's higher capex outlook bodes well for semi equipment makers
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) CEO Mark Murphy indicated during his company's second quarter fiscal 2026 earnings call that they plan to increase capital expenditures for the year by at least 25%, which should benefit semiconductor equipment manufacturers such as Applied Materials (AMAT), Lam Research (LRCX), and ASML (ASML).

"Yes, this FY '26, we increased our outlook on capex to over $25B, which was up from the $20B we did on the last earnings call," Murphy said. 

This increase is expected to go to investment in the Tongluo fab, expansion projects in the U.S., and equipment.

"Now, as we look out to '27, we did say that we project approximately a $10B incremental construction cost and also for equipment spend to increase," Murphy added. "Now that '27 spend, NAND will begin to increase, but it will still be a much smaller portion of the spend compared to DRAM."

Last week, Applied Materials announced a new partnership with Micron to develop and build DRAM, high-bandwidth memory, and NAND solutions to increase the performance of artificial intelligence systems. That same day, Applied Materials formed a similar partnership with another prolific memory maker, SK hynix.

"We expect to grow our semiconductor equipment business more than 20% this calendar year," said Applied Materials CEO Gary Dickerson. "We see the demand profile weighted towards the second half of the calendar year.''

Lam Research (LRCX) is also poised to benefit from the increase in spending. Lam is a primary provider of advanced deposition and etch equipment used by Micron to manufacture high-capacity memory chips. Last year, Micron presented Lam with the Outstanding Front End Equipment Supplier of the Year award.  

Finally, ASML Holding (ASML) is a primary provider of advanced lithography equipment to Micron. During its latest quarterly earnings report, ASML indicated that about 56% of its net bookings were for memory systems.

data.description:
The European Central Bank decided to keep the three key ECB interest rates unchanged on Thursday.

data.title: Crypto stocks plunge as rate cut hopes dampen
data.description:
Cryptocurrency stocks were trading lower after the Federal Reserve dampened rate cut expectations with a cautious commentary from Chair Jerome Powell.

The Fed's policymaking arm held its key interest rate at 3.50%-3.75%, as widely expected. The central bank made no changes to its median interest rate projections either, with a quarter-point reduction still penciled in for 2026.

With the FOMC keeping its key rate unchanged, Powell called the level "within plausible estimates of neutral"—the "point at which interest rates neither stimulate nor depress the economy.

There will be some effects on inflation coming forward, he said. But he expects to see more progress on inflation at about mid-year. "If we don't see inflation progress, you won't see a rate cut," Powell noted at his post-rate decision press conference.

Wall Street has stopped believing in an interest-rate cut this year by the Fed. Traders have now wiped out the last fully priced bet for a 2026 rate reduction.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices faced pressure after a brief market correction in the wake of the Fed announcement. BTC-USD slipped below the $70K level on Thursday before the U.S. market open. Ethereum (ETH-USD) was trading 3.32% lower at ~$2,130.

Crypto-linked stocks that were the worst affected include:

  • IREN (IREN) -4.36% to $40.37
  • Strategy (MSTR) -4.25% to $134.59
  • Hut 8 (HUT) -4.27% to $47.25
  • Circle Internet Group (CRCL) -4.16% to $127.31
  • Cipher Digital (CIFR) -3.82% to $14.11
  • Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) -3.77% to $32.41
  • Coinbase Global (COIN) -3.45% to $195.32
  • Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) -3.36% to $20.69
  • Robinhood Markets (HOOD) -3.20% to $72.50
  • MARA Holdings (MARA) -3.14% to $8.64
